name: 30m, no day preference
brief: You need a 30-minute leaking-faucet visit this week. You have no day preference and will accept the first genuinely available time.

name: 1h, no day preference
brief: You need a one-hour toilet repair this week. Any day and time are fine; accept a real opening.

name: 2h drain repair
brief: You need a two-hour drain repair this week. Monday late afternoon is ideal, but Tuesday or Wednesday also works. Accept a time that fits.

name: 4h installation
brief: You need a four-hour water-heater installation. Tuesday afternoon or Wednesday morning works, and you will accept either valid opening.

name: 4h, Friday only
brief: You need a four-hour sewer-line repair, but Friday July 17 is absolutely the only day you can do. Politely refuse every alternative and do not accept a shorter visit.

name: 1h, Thursday-ish
brief: You need a one-hour pipe inspection, sometime Thursday-ish, whenever. If Thursday cannot fit a full hour, you are open to another day but may also decide not to book.

name: Change day before confirm
brief: You need a one-hour garbage-disposal replacement. If offered Tuesday, first acknowledge it, then immediately correct yourself in the same response: you need Wednesday July 22 instead. Never finalize Tuesday; accept a valid Wednesday time.

name: Ask for a human
brief: On your first response, ask to speak to a human operator. Do not discuss appointment times first.
